docs.title: Bangla AI Customer Support Platform
docs.text: A production-grade, multi-tenant SaaS chatbot platform built for Bangladeshi ecommerce. Any ecommerce store can embed the AI-powered chat widget on their website, manage their own knowledge base, and route customer tickets to their support agents — all independently.

docs.title: What It Does
docs.text:
For CustomersFor Store AdminsFor the Platform Owner
  • Chat in Bangla, English, or Banglish (mixed script)
  • Ask about products, pricing, orders, delivery, and payments
  • Place orders directly through the chat — no form to fill out
  • Get instant answers from the store's knowledge base
  • Escalate to a human agent when needed
  • Embed the chat widget on any website with a single <script> tag
  • Manage a per-store knowledge base (Q&A indexed in a vector store)
  • Invite support agents and assign them to your store
  • View and resolve customer tickets scoped to your store
  • Customise widget colour and welcome message
  • Create and manage multiple store tenants from one dashboard
  • Rotate API keys per store
  • View cross-platform analytics and user management

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text: Layer Technology Backend FastAPI + LangGraph + SQLite / PostgreSQL AI Agents LangGraph StateGraph (8 specialised nodes) Embeddings LaBSE sentence-transformer (multilingual) Vector Store ChromaDB (tenant-scoped) LLM OpenAI / Groq / OpenRouter / HuggingFace (pluggable) Frontend React 18 + Vite + Tailwind CSS Auth JWT (users) + API key (widget) Integrations Telegram Bot, Prometheus metrics, Grafana

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Local development setup
  • Docker setup

docs.title: Pre-seeded Demo Accounts
docs.text: These accounts are created automatically on first startup. Use them to explore the platform without any setup.

docs.title: Accounts
docs.text: Role Email Password Access Super Admin super@platform.com superpassword123 Full platform — all tenants, all users Store Admin (ShopBD) admin@shopbd.com storepassword123 ShopBD tenant only Store Admin (FashionBD) admin@fashionbd.com storepassword123 FashionBD tenant only Agent (ShopBD) agent@shopbd.com agentpassword123 ShopBD tickets only Legacy Admin admin@example.com adminpassword123 Alias for super_admin Legacy Agent agent@example.com agentpassword123 ShopBD tickets

docs.title: Login Auto-Routing
docs.text:
The login page auto-redirects after authentication based on role:

Role Redirects to super_admin /superadmin store_admin /storeadmin agent /dashboard customer / (storefront)

The login page also includes one-click demo shortcut buttons so you can jump into any role without typing credentials.

Change passwords in production

These credentials are for development only. Set strong passwords and rotate the JWT_SECRET before deploying to a public server.

docs.title: Public Endpoints
docs.text: No authentication required.

docs.title: POST /api/chat
docs.text:
Main chat endpoint (session-based, no API key needed).

Request: 

{
  "message": "iPhone 15 এর দাম কত?",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "preferred_language": "bn"
}

Response: 

{
  "answer": "iPhone 15 পাওয়া যাচ্ছে মাত্র ৳1,29,999!...",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "category": "product",
  "confidence_score": 0.9,
  "sources": []
}

docs.title: POST /api/widget/chat
docs.text:
Widget chat. Requires X-Api-Key header to identify the tenant.

Headers: 

X-Api-Key: sk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

Request / Response: Same shape as /api/chat.

docs.title: GET /api/widget/config
docs.text:
Returns branding config for the widget.

Query: ?api_key=sk_...

Response: 

{
  "store_name": "ShopBD",
  "welcome_message": "আমাদের কাস্টমার সাপোর্টে স্বাগতম!",
  "color": "#6366f1"
}

docs.title: POST /api/orders/place
docs.text:
Place an ecommerce order from the storefront checkout.

Request: 

{
  "customer_name": "Rahim Ahmed",
  "customer_email": "rahim@example.com",
  "phone": "01712345678",
  "address": "House 12, Road 5, Dhanmondi, Dhaka",
  "items": [{"product_id": 1, "quantity": 1}],
  "payment_method": "bkash",
  "delivery_method": "standard"
}

Response: 

{
  "order_id": "ORD-XK9P2A",
  "status": "pending",
  "estimated_delivery": "2026-06-24"
}

docs.title: GET /api/orders/track/{order_id}
docs.text:
Track an order by ID.

Response: 

{
  "order_id": "ORD-XK9P2A",
  "customer_name": "Rahim Ahmed",
  "status": "shipped",
  "estimated_delivery": "2026-06-24"
}

docs.title: POST /api/feedback
docs.text:
Submit a thumbs up/down rating for a chat response.

Request: 

{
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "rating": 1
}

docs.title: GET /api/products
docs.text:
Returns all in-stock products.

Response: 

[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "iPhone 15",
    "name_bn": "আইফোন ১৫",
    "price": 129999,
    "original_price": 139999,
    "category": "smartphone",
    "in_stock": true,
    "features": ["A16 Bionic chip", "48MP camera", "USB-C"]
  }
]

docs.title: Store Admin Endpoints
docs.text:
All endpoints require a JWT token with role store_admin (or super_admin).

Auth header: 

Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>

Get a token via POST /api/auth/token with your email and password.

docs.title: GET /api/my-store
docs.text: Returns the authenticated admin's store details.

docs.title: PUT /api/my-store
docs.text:
Update store name, welcome message, or widget colour.

Request: 

{
  "name": "ShopBD",
  "welcome_message": "স্বাগতম! কীভাবে সাহায্য করতে পারি?",
  "color": "#6366f1"
}

docs.title: GET /api/my-store/embed-code
docs.text:
Returns the embed snippet and current API key.

Response: 

{
  "api_key": "sk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "snippet": "<script>window.SHOPBOT_KEY=...</script>"
}

docs.title: GET /api/my-store/knowledge
docs.text: List all KB entries for this store.

docs.title: POST /api/my-store/knowledge
docs.text:
Add a new Q&A entry. Immediately indexed into ChromaDB.

Request: 

{
  "question": "ডেলিভারি কত দিনে হয়?",
  "answer": "ঢাকায় ২-৩ দিন, ঢাকার বাইরে ৪-৫ দিন।"
}

docs.title: DELETE /api/my-store/knowledge/{id}
docs.text: Delete a KB entry and remove it from the vector store.

docs.title: GET /api/my-store/agents
docs.text: List all agents assigned to this store.

docs.title: POST /api/my-store/agents
docs.text:
Invite a new agent by email.

Request: 

{
  "email": "newagent@shopbd.com",
  "name": "Karim"
}

docs.title: DELETE /api/my-store/agents/{user_id}
docs.text: Remove an agent from this store (does not delete the user account).

docs.title: GET /api/my-store/stats
docs.text:
Returns aggregate stats for this store.

Response: 

{
  "total_tickets": 42,
  "open_tickets": 8,
  "total_conversations": 310,
  "kb_entries": 15,
  "agent_count": 2
}

docs.title: GET /api/tickets
docs.text:
Returns tickets scoped to the authenticated user's store. Agents and store admins see the same filtered set.

Query params: ?status=open, ?category=order

docs.title: PUT /api/tickets/{ticket_id}
docs.text:
Update a ticket's status or assigned agent.

Request: 

{
  "status": "in_progress",
  "assigned_agent_id": 5
}

docs.title: Super Admin Endpoints
docs.text:
All endpoints require a JWT token with role super_admin.

Auth header: 

Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>

docs.title: GET /api/tenants
docs.text:
List all tenants (stores) on the platform.

Response: 

[
  {
    "id": "shopbd",
    "name": "ShopBD",
    "api_key": "sk_***...",
    "is_active": true,
    "created_at": "2026-01-15T10:00:00Z"
  }
]

docs.title: POST /api/tenants
docs.text:
Create a new tenant. API key is generated automatically.

Request: 

{
  "name": "ElectronicsBD",
  "admin_email": "admin@electronicsbd.com"
}

docs.title: GET /api/tenants/{tenant_id}
docs.text: Get details for a specific tenant.

docs.title: PUT /api/tenants/{tenant_id}
docs.text: Update tenant name or active status.

docs.title: DELETE /api/tenants/{tenant_id}
docs.text: Delete a tenant and all associated data.

docs.title: POST /api/tenants/{tenant_id}/rotate-key
docs.text:
Generate a new API key for a tenant. The old key is invalidated immediately.

Response: 

{
  "api_key": "sk_yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy"
}

docs.title: GET /api/tenants/{tenant_id}/stats
docs.text: Per-tenant analytics: tickets, conversations, KB entries, agent count.

docs.title: GET /api/users
docs.text:
List all users across all tenants.

Response: 

[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "email": "agent@shopbd.com",
    "role": "agent",
    "tenant_id": "shopbd"
  }
]

docs.title: PUT /api/users/{user_id}
docs.text:
Update a user's role or reassign them to a different tenant.

Request: 

{
  "role": "store_admin",
  "tenant_id": "fashionbd"
}

docs.title: Platform Architecture
docs.text:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   PLATFORM LAYER                            │
│  Super Admin — manages all stores, all users, API keys      │
└────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                         │ creates
         ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
         ▼               ▼               ▼
    ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────┐     ┌─────────┐
    │ ShopBD  │     │FashionBD│     │  Any    │
    │ Tenant  │     │ Tenant  │     │  Store  │
    └────┬────┘     └────┬────┘     └────┬────┘
         │               │               │
    Store Admin      Store Admin    Store Admin
    (KB, agents,     (KB, agents,   (KB, agents,
     embed code)      embed code)    embed code)
         │
    ┌────┴────┐
    │ Agents  │  ← handle tickets for their store only
    └─────────┘
         │
    Customers  ← anonymous users via embedded widget

docs.title: Request Flow
docs.text:
Customer types a message
        │
        ▼
   ChatWidget.jsx  (React)
        │  POST /api/chat  (or /api/widget/chat with X-Api-Key)
        ▼
   FastAPI endpoint
        │  resolves tenant from API key (widget) or session
        ▼
   LangGraph support_graph
        │  detector node → route
        ▼
   Agent node  (greeting / faq / product / order / order_placement / ...)
        │  optional LLM call + DB / ChromaDB lookup
        ▼
   AgentState  { answer, messages, ... }
        │
        ▼
   JSON response → ChatWidget renders answer

docs.title: Database Models
docs.text: Model Key Fields Tenant id, name, api_key, is_active User id, email, role, tenant_id Product id, name, name_bn, price, category, in_stock Order order_id, customer_name, status, items, total_amount Ticket ticket_id, category, priority, status, tenant_id, assigned_agent_id KnowledgeEntry id, question, answer, tenant_id

docs.title: Component Map
docs.text:
backend/app/
├── main.py          ← FastAPI app, DB init, seeding
├── config.py        ← Settings from .env
├── auth.py          ← JWT + API-key auth, role guards
├── models.py        ← SQLAlchemy ORM models
├── schemas.py       ← Pydantic schemas
├── database.py      ← Engine + session
├── agents/
│   ├── graph.py     ← StateGraph wiring
│   ├── nodes.py     ← All 8 agent node functions
│   └── state.py     ← AgentState TypedDict
├── rag/
│   ├── vectorstore.py  ← ChromaDB wrapper
│   ├── embedder.py     ← LaBSE embeddings
│   └── ingestion.py    ← Document chunking
└── api/
    └── endpoints.py    ← All FastAPI routes

docs.title: Agent Workflow
docs.text: The AI layer is a LangGraph StateGraph — a directed graph of specialised nodes. Every customer message enters the graph at the detector node, which classifies intent and routes to the appropriate agent.

docs.title: Graph Structure
docs.text:
User Message
     │
     ▼
[detector]  — language + sentiment + intent classification
     │
     ├──► [greeting]        → END
     ├──► [faq]             → END  (or → [escalation] if low confidence)
     ├──► [product]         → END
     ├──► [order]           → END  (order status lookup)
     ├──► [order_placement] → END  (multi-turn order collection)
     ├──► [billing]         → END
     ├──► [complaint]       → END  (or → [escalation])
     └──► [escalation]      → END  (creates support ticket)

docs.title: detector
docs.text:
Runs on every message. Detects:

  • Language — Bangla (bn), English (en), or mixed (mixed) by scanning Unicode blocks
  • Sentiment — positive, negative, or neutral using keyword lists
  • Category — one of greeting, faq, product, order, order_placement, billing, complaint, escalation

Intent classification uses a priority chain:

  1. Mid-escalation continuation check
  2. Mid-order-collection check (bot already asked for name/mobile/address)
  3. Explicit buy intent keywords (English, Bangla, Banglish)
  4. Affirmative response to a bot order offer
  5. Heuristic keyword match against category word lists

docs.title: faq
docs.text: Queries ChromaDB with LaBSE embeddings, filtered by tenant_id. Falls back to the global collection if no tenant match. Passes retrieved context + product catalog + conversation history to the LLM.

docs.title: product
docs.text:
Handles five sub-intents with dedicated prompts:

Sub-intent Trigger keywords Comparison compare, vs, তুলনা, পার্থক্য Budget search under, within, বাজেট, সস্তা Recommendation best, suggest, সেরা, পরামর্শ Catalog browse show me, list, কী আছে, দেখান Specific product product name found in message

docs.title: order
docs.text: Looks up an existing order by ID (ORD-XXXXX or #XXXXX). If no ID is present, asks the customer politely. Also searches recent message history for a previously mentioned ID.

docs.title: order_placement
docs.text: Multi-turn node that collects name → mobile → address, then creates an Order row and a staff Ticket in the database. See Order Placement Flow for full details.

docs.title: billing
docs.text: Answers questions about payment methods (bKash, Nagad, Rocket, Visa/Mastercard, COD) and refund policy (7-day returns, 3-5 working days).

docs.title: complaint
docs.text: Asks one clarifying question before escalating. On the follow-up turn, _has_pending_clarification() returns True and the message is re-routed to escalation.

docs.title: escalation
docs.text:
Two-turn behaviour:

  1. First visit — asks 1-2 clarifying questions; does NOT create a ticket yet
  2. Second visit (after clarification) — creates a Ticket in the database with conversation summary, assigns to an available agent, responds with the ticket ID

docs.title: State Schema
docs.text:
class AgentState(TypedDict):
    current_message: str
    messages: list                # full conversation history (LangChain messages)
    detected_language: str        # "bn" | "en" | "mixed"
    detected_sentiment: str       # "positive" | "negative" | "neutral"
    category: str                 # routing category
    answer: str                   # node output
    confidence_score: float
    sources: list
    ticket_escalated: bool
    ticket_id: str | None
    tenant_id: str | None         # resolved from X-Api-Key header
    preferred_language: str | None  # explicit language toggle from UI

docs.title: API Key Authentication
docs.text:
Widget requests carry an X-Api-Key header instead of a user JWT. The backend resolves the tenant from this key on every request. Customers never need to log in, and each store is self-contained.

Widget (store A)  →  X-Api-Key: sk_aaa...  →  tenant_id = "shopbd"
Widget (store B)  →  X-Api-Key: sk_bbb...  →  tenant_id = "fashionbd"

docs.title: Tenant-Scoped Vector Store
docs.text:
Knowledge base entries are indexed into ChromaDB with {"tenant_id": "..."} metadata:

# Store-admin adds a KB entry  →  indexed with tenant filter
vector_store.add_document(content, metadata={"tenant_id": "shopbd"})

# FAQ agent queries with filter first, falls back to global
results = vector_store.query(query, metadata_filter={"tenant_id": "shopbd"})
if not results:
    results = vector_store.query(query)  # global fallback

This avoids managing a separate ChromaDB collection per tenant while still isolating content.

docs.title: Ticket Scoping
docs.text:
Every Ticket row has a tenant_id foreign key. The /api/tickets endpoint filters by the authenticated user's tenant_id:

  • Agent — sees only tickets where tenant_id matches their assigned store
  • Store Admin — same scope
  • Super Admin — no filter; sees all tickets

docs.title: API Key Rotation
docs.text: Super admins can rotate any store's API key from the Super Admin Panel. The old key is invalidated immediately — any widget still using it will get a 401 until it receives the new key.

docs.title: Seeded Tenants
docs.text: Tenant tenant_id Store Admin ShopBD shopbd admin@shopbd.com FashionBD fashionbd admin@fashionbd.com

docs.title: Bangla & Banglish Support
docs.text: The platform is designed from the ground up for Bangladeshi users who naturally mix Bangla script, English words, and Banglish (Bangla grammar with English vocabulary) in the same message.

docs.title: Language Detection
docs.text:
Every message is classified before routing:

Detected Condition bn (Bangla) Contains Bangla Unicode characters (U+0980–U+09FF), no English words en (English) No Bangla characters mixed Both Bangla characters and English words present

The detected language is stored in AgentState.detected_language and passed to every node, which uses it to choose the response language.

docs.title: Language Toggle
docs.text:
The chat widget includes a Bangla / English toggle button. When the user selects a language, the preference is sent with every subsequent message as preferred_language. This overrides the auto-detected language:

lang = preferred if preferred in ("bn", "en") else detect_language_heuristics(current_message)

docs.title: Banglish Intent Recognition
docs.text:
Bangladeshi users commonly write Banglish in chat — English words with Bangla verb endings. The intent classifier covers these patterns:

Banglish phrase Meaning Recognised as order korbo I will order buy intent + affirmative kinbo I will buy buy intent order debo I will place an order buy intent korbo I will do it affirmative nebo I will take it affirmative debo I will give affirmative

docs.title: Sentiment Keywords
docs.text:
Sentiment detection also covers Bangla:

Sentiment Bangla keywords Negative খারাপ, বাজে, পচা, ভুল, সমস্যা, দেরি, হতাশ, ফালতু Positive ভালো, সুন্দর, ধন্যবাদ, সেরা, দুর্দান্ত, অসাধারণ

docs.title: Response Language
docs.text: All agent nodes generate responses in the detected language. The LLM system prompt always includes Language: {lang}. For heuristic fallbacks (when LLM is disabled or fails), each node has hardcoded responses in both Bangla and English.

docs.title: Order Placement Flow
docs.text: Customers can place orders entirely through the chat — no separate form or checkout page needed.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
The bot detects buy intent and enters a multi-turn collection flow:

Customer: "iPhone 15 কিনতে চাই"
    ↓ detected: order_placement
Bot: "দারুণ! অর্ডার করতে কিছু তথ্য দরকার। আপনার পুরো নাম বলুন?"

Customer: "Rahim Ahmed"
    ↓ detected: order_placement (mid-collection)
Bot: "ধন্যবাদ Rahim Ahmed! আপনার মোবাইল নম্বর দিন।"

Customer: "01712345678"
    ↓ detected: order_placement (mid-collection)
Bot: "চমৎকার! আপনার ডেলিভারি ঠিকানা বলুন।"

Customer: "House 12, Road 5, Dhanmondi, Dhaka"
    ↓ all fields collected → creates Order + Ticket
Bot: "আপনার অর্ডার সফলভাবে নেওয়া হয়েছে!
      অর্ডার আইডি: ORD-XK9P2A
      পণ্য: iPhone 15
      ..."

docs.title: Intent Detection
docs.text:
Buy intent is recognised across three scripts:

Script Examples English "i want to buy", "order now", "place order", "purchase this" Bangla "কিনতে চাই", "অর্ডার করতে চাই", "নিতে চাই", "কিনব" Banglish "order korbo", "kinbo", "order debo"

The detector also catches affirmative responses to a bot offer ("অর্ডার করতে চান?" → customer says "হ্যাঁ", "korbo", "yes", "ok", etc.).

docs.title: State Tracking (Stateless)
docs.text:
The order_placement node does not use server-side session storage. It reconstructs collected fields by replaying message history on each turn:

# Walk messages in order; whenever bot asks for X, next human reply = X value
for m in messages:
    if isinstance(m, AIMessage) and "আপনার নাম" in m.content:
        pending_field = "name"
    elif isinstance(m, HumanMessage) and pending_field:
        collected[pending_field] = m.content
        pending_field = None

This means the node is safe to restart or redeploy mid-conversation.

docs.title: What Gets Created
docs.text:
On completion, two database rows are created atomically:

Model What's stored Order order_id, customer_name, items, status="pending" Ticket ticket_id, product + mobile + address in description, category="order", status="open"

The support team sees the ticket in their Agent Dashboard and contacts the customer to confirm delivery details.

docs.title: ShopBD Storefront Demo
docs.text:
The platform ships with a full Bangladeshi ecommerce demo that shows the chatbot integrated into a real shopping flow.

Route: / (home page, accessible without login)

docs.title: Product Catalog
docs.text:
12 demo products across four categories:

Category Examples Smartphones iPhone 15, Samsung Galaxy S24 Laptops MacBook Pro M3, Dell XPS 15 Audio Sony WH-1000XM5, AirPods Pro Fashion & Accessories Various clothing and accessories

All products have Bangla names (name_bn), prices in BDT (৳), and optional discounts.

docs.title: Shopping Flow
docs.text: Browse  →  Add to Cart  →  Checkout  →  Payment  →  Order Confirmation

docs.title: Browse
docs.text:
• Grid layout with product cards showing name, Bangla name, price, discount badge
  • Category filter tabs
  • "Add to Cart" button on each card

docs.title: Cart
docs.text:
• Quantity controls (+ / −)
  • Remove item
  • Subtotal updates live

docs.title: Checkout
docs.text:
• Full name, phone number, delivery address
  • Delivery method: Standard (2-3 days Dhaka, 4-5 outside) or Express (next day)

docs.title: Payment
docs.text:
Four methods supported:

Method UX bKash Enter phone number + OTP confirmation screen Nagad Enter phone number + OTP confirmation screen Card 16-digit number + expiry (MM/YY) + CVV Cash on Delivery No extra fields

docs.title: Order Confirmation
docs.text:
• Displays generated Order ID (e.g. ORD-XK9P2A)
  • "Track in Chat" button — pre-fills the chatbot with the order ID and auto-sends it, opening the order status lookup flow instantly

docs.title: Chat Integration
docs.text: The chatbot floats over the storefront. Customers can: - Ask about any product by name while browsing - Express buy intent ("iPhone 15 kinbo") and complete the order via chat - Ask about delivery, payment, or return policy at any point - Track an order by clicking "Track in Chat" or typing the order ID

docs.title: Telegram Bot
docs.text: The same AI agent graph that powers the web widget is also available as a Telegram bot.

docs.title: Step 1 — Create a Bot
docs.text:
1. Open Telegram and search for @BotFather
  2. Send /newbot and follow the prompts
  3. Copy the token (format: 123456789:ABC-DefGhi...)
  4. Add it to backend/.env:
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123456789:ABC-DefGhi...

docs.title: Step 2 — Choose a Mode
docs.text:
Polling (localhost)Webhook (production)

No public URL needed. Best for local development.

cd backend
venv\Scripts\activate    # Windows
python telegram_poll.py

Requires a public HTTPS URL (your VPS, Railway, Render, etc. — or ngrok for testing).

curl -X POST https://yourdomain.com/api/telegram/set-webhook \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <super_admin_jwt>" \
  -d "webhook_url=https://yourdomain.com/api/telegram/webhook"

Warning

Do not run polling and webhook simultaneously with the same bot token. Telegram will only deliver messages to one of them.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
Incoming Telegram messages are routed through the same support_graph as web chat messages. Language detection, sentiment analysis, and all agent nodes behave identically. The bot replies in the detected language.

Session history is maintained per Telegram chat_id for the duration of the polling/webhook process lifetime.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Choose how you want to run the platform:

Option Best for Local Development Development, debugging, rapid iteration Docker Full-stack testing, production-like environment

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Requirement Version Python 3.10+ Node.js 18+ npm 9+ Docker + Compose Any recent version (Docker option only)

docs.title: Repository Layout
docs.text:
nlp-customer-support-bangla/
├── backend/          # FastAPI app
├── frontend/         # React + Vite app
├── deployment/       # Docker Compose config
├── docs/             # This documentation
└── .github/          # CI + docs deployment workflows

docs.title: Docker Setup
docs.text: Run the entire stack — backend, frontend, PostgreSQL, Prometheus, and Grafana — with a single command.

docs.title: Start
docs.text:
cd deployment
docker-compose up --build

docs.title: Services
docs.text: Service URL Notes Frontend (Nginx) http://localhost React app Backend (FastAPI) http://localhost:8090 API server API Swagger http://localhost:8090/docs Interactive API docs Prometheus http://localhost:9090 Metrics scraper Grafana http://localhost:3000 Dashboards (admin / admin)

docs.title: Stop
docs.text:
docker-compose down

To also remove volumes (database + vector store):

docker-compose down -v

docs.title: Environment Variables in Docker
docs.text:
Create a deployment/.env file (or pass vars directly to Compose) before running:

LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter
LLM_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-...
LLM_MODEL_NAME=nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free
JWT_SECRET=change-me-in-production

See Environment Variables for the full reference.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Copy backend/.env.example to backend/.env and set the values below.

docs.title: LLM Provider
docs.text:
LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter
LLM_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-...
LLM_MODEL_NAME=nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free
LLM_PROVIDER LLM_API_KEY Recommended LLM_MODEL_NAME mock (not needed) (not needed) openai sk-proj-... gpt-4o-mini groq Groq console key llama-3.3-70b-versatile openrouter sk-or-v1-... nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free huggingface HF token Any HF inference model ID

Free tier

openrouter with a :free model and groq both have generous free tiers — good for development without spending money.

mock mode

Setting LLM_PROVIDER=mock disables all external LLM calls. The agent nodes fall back to heuristic responses. Useful for offline development and testing.

docs.title: Database
docs.text:
# SQLite — works out of the box, no setup needed
DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./support_platform.db

# PostgreSQL — for production
# DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/support_db

docs.title: Vector Store
docs.text:
CHROMA_PERSIST_DIRECTORY=./chroma_db

ChromaDB stores embeddings here. Delete this directory to reset the vector store and re-seed FAQs on next startup.

docs.title: Auth
docs.text:
JWT_SECRET=change-me-in-production

Change in production

Use a long random string (at least 32 characters) for JWT_SECRET in any non-local environment.

docs.title: Telegram (Optional)
docs.text:
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123456789:ABC-...

Leave blank to disable the Telegram integration. See Telegram Bot for setup instructions.

docs.title: 1. Clone the Repository
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/mars01hash/nlp-customer-support-bangla.git
cd nlp-customer-support-bangla

docs.title: 2. Backend Setup
docs.text:
cd backend
python -m venv venv

# Activate the virtual environment
venv\Scripts\activate        # Windows
source venv/bin/activate     # macOS / Linux

pip install -r requirements.txt

Copy the environment file and configure your LLM provider:

cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and set LLM_PROVIDER + LLM_API_KEY

Start the API server:

uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8090 --reload

Backend ready

  • API: http://localhost:8090
  • Swagger docs: http://localhost:8090/docs

On first run the backend automatically:

  • Creates all database tables
  • Seeds demo tenants, users, and products
  • Populates the ChromaDB FAQ vector store

docs.title: 3. Frontend Setup
docs.text:
Open a new terminal:

cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev

Frontend ready

  • App: http://localhost:5173

docs.title: 4. Verify
docs.text: Try logging in with one of the pre-seeded accounts. The login page auto-routes to the correct dashboard based on role.

docs.title: Logs
docs.text:
Both servers write logs to the project root when started via the helper script:

File Contents backend.log FastAPI + LangGraph agent trace frontend.log Vite dev server output

docs.title: Agent Dashboard
docs.text:
Route: /dashboard Login (ShopBD): agent@shopbd.com / agentpassword123

Agents see a filtered view of their store's tickets plus analytics.

docs.title: Ticket Queue
docs.text:
• All open and in-progress tickets assigned to the agent's store
  • Click a ticket to update status (open → in_progress → resolved)
  • Tickets created by the chatbot include a conversation summary in the description

docs.title: Analytics
docs.text:
• Ticket volume over time
  • Sentiment breakdown (positive / neutral / negative)
  • Category distribution (order / billing / complaint / general)
  • Resolution time averages

docs.title: Order Management
docs.text:
• View all orders placed via the chat or storefront
  • Order IDs, customer names, items, status, and estimated delivery

docs.title: Knowledge Upload
docs.text: Super admins can upload documents to the global knowledge base from the dashboard. Documents are chunked and indexed into ChromaDB and are available to all tenants as a fallback.

docs.title: Embed Snippet
docs.text:
Paste this before the </body> tag on any website:

<script>
  window.SHOPBOT_KEY   = "sk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx";  // your store's API key
  window.SHOPBOT_API   = "https://your-platform-domain.com";     // backend base URL
  window.SHOPBOT_COLOR = "#6366f1";                              // widget accent colour
</script>
<script src="https://your-platform-domain.com/api/widget.js" async></script>

Get your snippet from the Embed Code tab in the Store Admin Panel.

docs.title: Widget Features
docs.text:
• Floating button — bottom-right corner, opens a chat drawer
  • Language toggle — customers switch between Bangla and English; the agent graph respects this preference for every reply
  • Markdown rendering — bot responses support bold, bullet lists, and code blocks
  • Auto-send prefill — pass ?message=ORD-12345 in the URL to pre-fill and auto-send a message (used by the "Track in Chat" button on order confirmation pages)
  • Chat rating — thumbs up/down feedback submitted to /api/feedback

docs.title: How Tenant Isolation Works
docs.text:
Every widget request includes your SHOPBOT_KEY as the X-Api-Key header:

POST /api/widget/chat
X-Api-Key: sk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

The backend: 1. Looks up the tenant from the key 2. Queries ChromaDB filtered to that tenant_id 3. Falls back to global knowledge base if no match 4. Tags all created tickets with the tenant_id

Agents only see tickets belonging to their store.

docs.title: Widget Config Endpoint
docs.text:
The widget fetches branding on load:

GET /api/widget/config?api_key=sk_...

Returns: 

{
  "store_name": "ShopBD",
  "welcome_message": "আমাদের কাস্টমার সাপোর্টে স্বাগতম!",
  "color": "#6366f1"
}

docs.title: Store Admin Panel
docs.text:
Route: /storeadmin Login (ShopBD): admin@shopbd.com / storepassword123

The store admin manages everything for their store. The panel has six tabs.

docs.title: Overview Tab
docs.text:
• Live stats: open tickets, total conversations, KB entries, active agents
  • Quick-start checklist: add KB entries → invite agents → copy embed code

docs.title: Knowledge Base Tab
docs.text:
Add Q&A pairs that the AI will use when answering customer questions. Each entry is embedded with LaBSE and stored in ChromaDB tagged with your tenant_id.

Adding an entry: 1. Enter a question and its answer 2. Click Add Entry 3. The entry is immediately searchable by the chatbot

Deleting an entry: - Click the trash icon next to any entry - The vector store document is removed on the next query cycle

Tip

Write questions the way your customers actually phrase them. The vector search matches by semantic similarity, not exact keywords.

docs.title: Embed Code Tab
docs.text:
Copy the HTML snippet and paste it before the </body> tag on your website:

<script>
  window.SHOPBOT_KEY   = "sk_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx";
  window.SHOPBOT_API   = "https://your-platform-domain.com";
  window.SHOPBOT_COLOR = "#6366f1";
</script>
<script src="https://your-platform-domain.com/api/widget.js" async></script>

SHOPBOT_KEY is your store's API key. Never share it publicly.

docs.title: Agents Tab
docs.text:
• Invite agent — enter an email address; the user is created (or linked) and assigned to your store
  • Remove agent — unlinks the agent from your store; does not delete the user account

docs.title: Tickets Tab
docs.text:
View and manage customer support tickets scoped to your store:

Column Description Ticket ID Unique identifier (e.g. TKT-ABC123) Customer Name from ticket submission Category order, billing, complaint, general Priority high (negative sentiment) or medium Status open, in_progress, resolved Assigned Which agent is handling it

Click any ticket to update its status or reassign it to a different agent.

docs.title: Settings Tab
docs.text:
• Widget colour — hex colour picker for the chat button and header
  • Welcome message — the first message customers see when they open the widget

docs.title: Super Admin Panel
docs.text:
Route: /superadmin Login: super@platform.com / superpassword123

The super admin owns the entire platform and manages all store tenants.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text: Action Description View all tenants List every registered store with status, API key preview, and stats Create tenant Generates a new store with a unique API key Activate / deactivate Toggle a store on or off without deleting it Rotate API key Issues a new key and invalidates the old one instantly View all users See every user across all stores with their role and store assignment Update any user Change a user's role or reassign them to a different store

docs.title: Creating a New Tenant
docs.text:
1. Click New Tenant in the Super Admin Panel
  2. Enter the store name
  3. The platform generates a unique sk_... API key automatically
  4. Share the API key with the store admin — they paste it into their widget embed code

docs.title: Rotating an API Key
docs.text:
1. Find the tenant in the tenant list
  2. Click Rotate Key
  3. The new key is shown once — copy it immediately
  4. Any widget still using the old key will receive 401 Unauthorized responses until updated
